schedules.label: Review tasks every 20 minutes
schedules.description: Periodically wakes the agent to review its open tasks: any in_progress that's stalled? any pending that's blocked? any completed that's actually still pending? Self-correcting accountability so work doesn't quietly stop.
schedules.prompt:
Standup with yourself. Walk every task on your panel:

1. in_progress with movement since last review → keep going, no message.
2. in_progress with NO movement → take the next concrete action RIGHT NOW. If you cannot, demote to pending with a one-line reason in the description (e.g. 'blocked: waiting on CI for PR #42'). Do not leave a stalled in_progress in place.
3. Delegated tasks (description has 'owner: <worker-id>') → run `roster describe <worker-id>` + `roster trace <worker-id> --tail 20`. If the worker is stalled or off-track, ping them with a follow-up, or interrupt and re-task. If they finished, mark the task completed and report.
4. pending no longer relevant → delete.
5. completed but the user is waiting → report it now.

The review is worthless if you finish it without taking any action. Convert 'I will get to it' into 'I am doing it now / it is blocked on X / it is no longer mine' on every fire.
